file_name: Monsanto Company - SECOND A_R EXCLUSIVE AGENCY AND MARKETING AGREEMENT .txt

perturbation.original_text: In consideration to the Agent for performance of its duties and obligations hereunder, the Agent shall be entitled to a Commission ("Commission"). Such Commission shall represent a percentage of the Program EBIT realized by the Roundup L&G Business which percentage shall be (i) for Program Years 2017 and 2018, 50% of the Program EBIT and (ii) for Program Years 2019 and thereafter, 50% of the Program EBIT in excess of $40MM (such $40MM threshold, the "Commission Threshold"). The parties agree that the Commission Threshold may be amended from time to time by mutual agreement of the parties following the inclusion or exclusion of either new or existing countries in the Included Markets, including Activated Included Markets, or Excluded Markets, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration to the Agent for performance of its duties and obligations hereunder, the Agent shall be entitled to a Commission ("Commission"). Such Commission shall represent a percentage of the Program EBIT realized by the Roundup L&G Business which percentage shall be (i) for Program Years 2017 and 2018, 50% of the Program EBIT and (ii) for Program Years 2019 and thereafter, 50% of the Program EBIT in excess of $40MM (such $40MM threshold, the "Commission Threshold"). The parties agree that the Commission Threshold may be amended from time to time by mutual agreement of the parties following the inclusion or exclusion of either new or existing countries in the Included Markets, including Activated Included Markets, or Excluded Markets, as applicable. **Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, the Commission Threshold shall unilaterally be amended by Monsanto and at its sole discretion.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a stark in-text contradiction regarding the Commission Threshold. The original text states that it "may be amended from time to time by mutual agreement of the parties." The added sentence, however, states that the Commission Threshold shall be unilaterally amended by Monsanto at its sole discretion. This directly contradicts the mutual agreement clause and could cause uncertainty as to the enforceability of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration to the Agent for performance of its duties and obligations hereunder, the Agent shall be entitled to a Commission ("Commission"). Such Commission shall represent a percentage of the Program EBIT realized by the Roundup L&G Business which percentage shall be (i) for Program Years 2017 and 2018, 50% of the Program EBIT and (ii) for Program Years 2019 and thereafter, 50% of the Program EBIT in excess of $40MM (such $40MM threshold, the "Commission Threshold"). The parties agree that the Commission Threshold may be amended from time to time by mutual agreement of the parties following the inclusion or exclusion of either new or existing countries in the Included Markets, including Activated Included Markets, or Excluded Markets, as applicable. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, the Commission Threshold shall unilaterally be amended by Monsanto and at its sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that the Commission Threshold may be amended by 'mutual agreement of the parties.' The changed text adds a sentence stating that the Commission Threshold shall be unilaterally amended by Monsanto at its sole discretion. This directly conflicts with the mutual agreement clause, creating a clear contradiction and uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the contract, as it now contains two incompatible methods for amending the threshold. The contradicted text is the original clause stating mutual agreement is required.

perturbation.original_text: The Agent shall have the right to delegate part of its obligations under this Article 2 to sub-agents and sub-distributors; provided, however, the Agent shall remain primarily liable for all of its obligations hereunder and shall be primarily liable for any act or omission of any such sub-agent or sub-distributor. To the extent this Agreement creates any obligations on the Agent, such obligations shall apply with respect to any sub-agents or sub- distributors, as the case may be. In connection with the foregoing, any reports or other information to be given to Monsanto shall be given by the Agent and shall include any information applicable to sub-agents or sub-distributors, as the case may be.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agent shall have the right to delegate part of its obligations under this Article 2 to sub-agents and sub-distributors; provided, however, the Agent shall remain primarily liable for all of its obligations hereunder and shall be primarily liable for any act or omission of any such sub-agent or sub-distributor. To the extent this Agreement creates any obligations on the Agent, such obligations shall apply with respect to any sub-agents or sub- distributors, as the case may be. **Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, the Agent shall have no liability for the actions of their sub-agents or sub-distributors.** In connection with the foregoing, any reports or other information to be given to Monsanto shall be given by the Agent and shall include any information applicable to sub-agents or sub-distributors, as the case may be.
perturbation.explanation: The core contradiction lies in the original clause asserting the Agent's primary liability for its sub-agents, which is directly negated by the perturbed sentence saying: "Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, the Agent shall have no liability for the actions of their sub-agents or sub-distributors." This introduces legal ambiguity because the contractual liability of the Agent for delegated obligations is explicitly denied, contradicting the provision's initial intent. It creates uncertainty on who is legally accountable for the fulfillment of contracted duties when the Agent utilizes sub-agents, especially when there are acts or omissions. This can lead to disputes over contract compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Agent shall have the right to delegate part of its obligations under this Article 2 to sub-agents and sub-distributors; provided, however, the Agent shall remain primarily liable for all of its obligations hereunder and shall be primarily liable for any act or omission of any such sub-agent or sub-distributor. To the extent this Agreement creates any obligations on the Agent, such obligations shall apply with respect to any sub-agents or sub- distributors, as the case may be. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, the Agent shall have no liability for the actions of their sub-agents or sub-distributors. In connection with the foregoing, any reports or other information to be given to Monsanto shall be given by the Agent and shall include any information applicable to sub-agents or sub-distributors, as the case may be.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the initial clause. The original text states the Agent remains primarily liable for the acts of sub-agents/distributors. The changed text states that 'the Agent shall have no liability for the actions of their sub-agents or sub-distributors.' This directly negates the agent's previously assigned liability.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge that Monsanto currently sells Glyphosate-based products under the Roundup trademark, directly or indirectly, to professional, industrial and agricultural users ("Roundup Ag Products"). Monsanto acknowledges that one of such Roundup Ag Products, the 2.5 gallon SKU containing 41% concentration of Glyphosate with the Brand name Roundup Pro (the "Roundup Pro SKU"), is currently being sold through Lawn and Garden Channels in the United States and may be purchased by consumers in the Lawn and Garden Market. Monsanto also acknowledges its obligations pursuant to Section 6.13(b) hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that Monsanto currently sells Glyphosate-based products under the Roundup trademark, directly or indirectly, to professional, industrial and agricultural users ("Roundup Ag Products"). Monsanto acknowledges that one of such Roundup Ag Products, the 2.5 gallon SKU containing 41% concentration of Glyphosate with the Brand name Roundup Pro (the "Roundup Pro SKU"), is currently being sold through Lawn and Garden Channels in the United States and may be purchased by consumers in the Lawn and Garden Market. **Notwithstanding the foregoing, Monsanto will now sell, directly and without limitation, the Roundup Pro SKU in Lawn and Garden Channels in the United States, waiving any obligations stated in Section 6.13(b).** Monsanto also acknowledges its obligations pursuant to Section 6.13(b) hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The contradiction created here revolves around the intent of Monsanto's sales and its obligations, especially conflicting with Section 6.13(b). It is initially acknowledged that Monsanto's product (Roundup Pro SKU) is sold via certain channels and that Monsanto is aware of Section 6.13(b). The inserted text then explicitly states: "Notwithstanding the foregoing, Monsanto will now sell, directly and without limitation, the Roundup Pro SKU in Lawn and Garden Channels in the United States, waiving any obligations stated in Section 6.13(b)." This phrase directly negates previous statements about acknowledging its obligations as per the original Section 6.13(b) of the text, directly influencing how the obligations will be fulfilled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that Monsanto currently sells Glyphosate-based products under the Roundup trademark, directly or indirectly, to professional, industrial and agricultural users ("Roundup Ag Products"). Monsanto acknowledges that one of such Roundup Ag Products, the 2.5 gallon SKU containing 41% concentration of Glyphosate with the Brand name Roundup Pro (the "Roundup Pro SKU"), is currently being sold through Lawn and Garden Channels in the United States and may be purchased by consumers in the Lawn and Garden Market. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Monsanto will now sell, directly and without limitation, the Roundup Pro SKU in Lawn and Garden Channels in the United States, waiving any obligations stated in Section 6.13(b). Monsanto also acknowledges its obligations pursuant to Section 6.13(b) hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that Monsanto acknowledges its obligations pursuant to Section 6.13(b). The changed text then explicitly states that Monsanto will waive any obligations stated in Section 6.13(b). This directly negates the initial acknowledgement of those obligations, creating a clear conflict within the same section of the document, thus creating a contradiction.
